meta.title: Managing Content in Strapi: A Non-Technical Guide
meta.excerpt: Learn how to create, edit, and publish content in Strapi without touching any code. This guide is written for business administrators and content editors who manage website content.

props.content: Strapi is a headless CMS (Content Management System) that lets you create, edit, and publish website content without touching any code. Think of it as a structured content editor -- like a more powerful version of a word processor, but designed specifically for website content.

A traditional CMS (like WordPress) controls both the content AND how it looks. A headless CMS like Strapi only manages the content -- the website's design is handled separately by the development team. This means you can focus entirely on writing great content without worrying about breaking the design.

props.title: How Content Flows from Strapi to the Website

metrics.label: Content Types
metrics.description: Pages, posts, services, FAQs, testimonials, team

props.content: Every piece of content in Strapi follows a defined structure called a content type. Think of it like a form template -- each content type has specific fields that you fill in. This ensures consistency across the website.

features.title: Single Types
features.description: One-off pages like the Homepage, About page, or Contact page. There is only one instance of each.

features.title: Collection Types
features.description: Repeatable content like blog posts, services, FAQs, or team members. You can create as many as needed.

features.title: Components
features.description: Reusable content blocks like a CTA section, image gallery, or pricing card that can be used across multiple pages.

features.title: Media Library
features.description: Centralised storage for all images, PDFs, and documents. Upload once, use across any content type.

props.title: Content Type Reference
rows: Title, Description, Price Range, Image, Category
Title, Body, Author, Featured Image, Tags, Excerpt
How do I reset my circuit breaker?
Customer Name, Quote, Rating, Service Type, Date
Hero Title, Hero Image, Featured Services, CTA Text

steps.title: Log In
steps.description: Access Strapi admin at /admin

steps.title: Navigate
steps.description: Find your content type in the sidebar

steps.title: Create/Edit
steps.description: Fill in or update fields

steps.title: Preview
steps.description: Check your changes in preview

steps.title: Publish
steps.description: Make it live on the website

steps.title: Draft
steps.description: Content is being written or edited. Not visible on the website. Only you and other admins can see it in Strapi.

steps.title: In Review
steps.description: Content is complete and ready for someone else to check. Use this if your team has an approval process.

steps.title: Published
steps.description: Content is live on the website. Visitors can see it. Changes to published content create a new draft version.

steps.title: Unpublished
steps.description: Previously published content that has been taken offline. The content still exists in Strapi but is hidden from the website.

props.content: When you edit a published piece of content, your changes are NOT immediately visible on the website. Strapi creates a draft version of your changes. You must click 'Publish' again to make the updated content live. This protects you from accidentally publishing incomplete edits.

steps.title: Navigate to the Content Type
steps.description: In the left sidebar, click 'Content Manager', then select the content type you want to create (e.g., 'Services', 'Blog Posts').

steps.title: Click 'Create New Entry'
steps.description: The blue button in the top-right corner opens a new blank form with all the required fields for this content type.

steps.title: Fill In Required Fields
steps.description: Fields marked with a red asterisk (*) are required. You cannot save or publish without completing these.

steps.title: Add Media
steps.description: Click any image field to open the Media Library. You can upload new images or select from existing ones. Always add alt text.

steps.title: Set the Slug
steps.description: The slug is the URL-friendly version of your title. Auto-generated but you can customise it. Never change after publishing.

steps.title: Save as Draft
steps.description: Click 'Save' to store your work as a draft. You can come back and continue editing at any time.

steps.title: Preview (Optional)
steps.description: If preview is configured, click the eye icon to see how your content will look on the live website.

steps.title: Publish
steps.description: When you are satisfied with the content, click 'Publish'. The content will be live on the website within a few minutes.

props.content: Never change the slug of a published page. The slug is part of the URL (e.g., yoursite.com/services/emergency-repair). Changing it breaks all existing links, bookmarks, and search engine rankings. If you must change a slug, ask a developer to set up a redirect first.

Click any content entry in the list view to open it for editing. Make your changes, then decide whether to save as draft (for later) or publish immediately. Remember: editing a published entry creates a draft version -- the live content stays unchanged until you explicitly publish.

features.title: Bulk Publish
features.description: Select multiple entries using checkboxes, then click 'Publish' to make them all live at once. Useful for launching a new section.

features.title: Bulk Unpublish
features.description: Take multiple entries offline simultaneously. Use this for seasonal content (e.g., hiding Christmas promotions in January).

features.title: Bulk Delete
features.description: Permanently remove multiple entries. This cannot be undone -- use with extreme caution. Consider unpublishing instead.

features.title: Export
features.description: Download content as JSON or CSV for backup, migration, or external review. Always export before making large changes.

steps.title: Upload
steps.description: Drag and drop or browse

steps.title: Organise
steps.description: Create folders by category

steps.title: Optimise
steps.description: Resize and compress

steps.title: Tag
steps.description: Add alt text and captions

steps.title: Use
steps.description: Insert into content entries

props.content: Large images slow down the website. Before uploading, resize your images to the recommended dimensions and compress them using a free tool like TinyPNG (tinypng.com) or Squoosh (squoosh.app). A 5 MB photo can usually be compressed to under 200 KB without visible quality loss.

props.title: Which Content Type Should I Use?

decisions.result: Use a Single Type (Homepage, About, Contact)
Use a Collection Type (Services, Posts, FAQs)

decisions.condition: Reusable block used in multiple places?
decisions.result: Use a Component (CTA block, Image Gallery)

decisions.condition: Not sure what structure to use?
decisions.result: Ask a developer to help define the structure

steps.title: Go to Content Manager > Services
steps.description: Click 'Create new entry'

steps.title: Enter the Service Name
steps.description: This becomes the page heading on the website

steps.title: Write the Description
steps.description: Use the rich text editor. Include what the service covers, typical timeframes, and pricing guidance

steps.title: Set the Price Range
steps.description: E.g., 'From R150/hour' or 'Quotation required'

steps.title: Upload a Service Image
steps.description: Choose a relevant, high-quality photo. Add alt text describing the image

steps.title: Select the Category
steps.description: Choose from existing categories (Residential, Commercial, Emergency, etc.)

steps.title: Save and Publish
steps.description: The new service will appear on the Services page automatically

steps.title: Go to Content Manager > FAQs
steps.description: Find the question you want to update or click 'Create new entry'

steps.title: Edit the Question and Answer
steps.description: Keep answers concise -- 2-3 paragraphs maximum. Use plain language

steps.title: Set the Sort Order
steps.description: Lower numbers appear first. Use increments of 10 (10, 20, 30) so you can insert new items between existing ones

steps.title: Assign a Category
steps.description: Group related FAQs together (Pricing, Safety, Emergency, General)

steps.title: Publish
steps.description: Updated FAQ appears on the website within minutes

props.title: Common Issues and Solutions
rows: Check the status badge -- click 'Publish' if it shows 'Draft'
Wait 5-10 minutes. If still not visible, ask a developer to clear the cache
File too large or wrong format
Compress the image and ensure it is JPG, PNG, WebP, or SVG
Slugs cannot be changed after publishing. Contact a developer
Clear formatting first: select all text, click 'Clear Formatting', then re-apply styles in Strapi
Unpublish first, then check for references in other content types before deleting

features.title: Write for the Web
features.description: Use short paragraphs (2-3 sentences max), descriptive headings, and bullet points. Web visitors scan -- they don't read every word.

features.title: Always Add Alt Text
features.description: Every image needs alt text that describes what the image shows. This helps visually impaired visitors and improves SEO rankings.

features.title: Save Often
features.description: Strapi does not auto-save. Get in the habit of clicking 'Save' every few minutes while editing long content.

features.title: Use Preview Before Publishing
features.description: Always preview your changes on the actual website before hitting Publish. What looks good in the Strapi editor may need adjustments on the live site.

features.title: Keep Media Organised
features.description: Create folders in the Media Library by category (services, team, blog, general). This saves time when you have hundreds of images.

features.title: Never Change Published Slugs
features.description: The slug is the URL. Changing it breaks links, bookmarks, and search rankings. If a slug must change, ask a developer to set up a redirect first.

props.content: Strapi manages content, not design -- focus on writing quality content and the website handles the presentation. Always publish to make changes live -- saved drafts are only visible to admins in Strapi. Never change slugs after publishing -- it breaks all existing links to that page. Optimise images before uploading -- compress to under 300 KB for fast page loads. Use the draft/publish workflow -- save early, preview before publishing, and never rush content live.
